title: HMA determination

steps: Inoculate 5 ml of LB medium with 1% volume of E. coli culture and culture at 37℃ for 12h. 200 rpm, 37 °C, 12 hours Antibiotics of corresponding resistance should be added into the LB medium.

Prepare ZYM-5052 medium (5ml) by mix ingredients below: (for detailed recipe ZYM-5052.docx A B 1 ZY 4800μl 2 50×M 100μl 3 50×5052 100μl 4 1000×Trace element 5μl/10μl 5 1M MgSO4·7H2O 5μl/10μl 6 antibiotic 5μl 7 20% arabinose 50μl
Inoculate 5 ml of ZYM-5052 medium with 1% volume of E. coli LB culture and culture at 37℃ for 12h. 200 rpm, 37 °C, 12 hours
Prepare M9 medium (10ml) by mix ingredients below: A B 1 5 × M9 2ml 2 1M MgSO4·7H2O 20μl 3 1 M CaCl2 1μl 4 20% glucose 200μl 5 ddH2O 7.8ml
Centrifuge 6OD bacteria in 1.5 ml eppendorf (EP) tube at 4,200 x rpm for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Decant or aspirate and discard the culture media.
Use 200μl M9 medium to spirate and dis completely resuspend cell pellet and culture at 37℃ for 12h. 200 rpm, 37 °C, 12 hours
Centrifuge at 15,000 x g for 10 minute at room temperature.
Take 100 µl of supernatant into another EP tube and mix it with 900μl ddH2O.
Filter the sample with 0.22μm membrane filters and inject the sample into a clean sample bottle.

HMA was measured an HPLC (Agilent 1260) equipped with a UV detector and a Welch Xtimate C8 column (4.6 × 250 mm, 3.5 µm). The samples were analyzed under the following gradient of eluent A (8 mM KH2PO4, pH 2.4) and eluent B (100% acetonitril): 5 min 10% B, 20 min linear gradient to 30% B, 3 min linear gradient to 50% B, 5 min 50% B, 2 min linear gradient to 10% B, 5 min 10% B. HMA was detected at 215 nm.
creator: Shuning Guo
